what_is_open: Whether the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule actually attains the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax value R_exact(beta,k,q), not only the TV-envelope rate.
obstruction: The frozen primitives provide the round-mean variance envelope Var_pi(bar_Y_j)<=sigma_0^2/n, which upper-bounds every exact covariance risk by the l1 amplification A_beta(p). They do not provide a converse covariance construction or a characterization of the attainable matrices Gamma_P(p) under monotone Bernoulli rollout. Without such a lower-envelope or least-favorable finite-population construction, exact optimality of p^Ch for R_exact cannot be derived from the available primitives.
attempted: The TV-envelope route closes completely: lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper transfers the Chebyshev upper bound for A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) to the exact risk. Attempts to compare R_exact from below with a positive multiple of A_beta(p), or to realize the rank-one worst-case covariance used by the TV envelope inside P_beta for every schedule, require covariance attainability assumptions not present in the frozen core.
partial_result: lem:exact-chebyshev-rate-feasible proves that for beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), q in (0,q_max], and rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2, inf_{w in W_beta(p^Ch(k,q))} sup_{P in P_beta} w' Gamma_P(p^Ch(k,q)) w <= (sigma_0^2/n) C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta), and hence R_exact(beta,k,q) <= (sigma_0^2/n) C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Thus Chebyshev-Lobatto is exact-risk rate-feasible at the TV-envelope minimax exponent and pointwise base; exact optimizer status remains open.
